section: The influence of silicon and aluminum alloying on the lattice parameter and stacking fault energy of austenitic steel

context_update.summary: This chunk presents core experimental data for the effects of 2.5 wt.% Si and Al on austenite lattice parameter and SFE in three fully austenitic Fe-Ni-Cr alloys: Base, 2.5 Si, and 2.5 Al. Processing included vacuum casting, homogenization (1200°C/20 h), hot rolling, and annealing to ~140 μm grain size. XRD (Cu Kα, extrapolation) yielded lattice parameters: base 0.3585 nm, 2.5 Si 0.3587 nm, 2.5 Al 0.3598 nm, corresponding to a 1.41×10⁻³ nm expansion by Al and negligible Si effect. Linear lattice parameter coefficients were derived; ignoring Al leads to carbon content overestimation of 0.125–0.176 wt.% for 1 wt.% Al. SFE was determined by WBDF TEM of dissociated dislocations in 5% pre-strained samples; uncorrected SFE values: base 26.3 mJ/m², 2.5 Al 38.3 mJ/m² (+12.0), 2.5 Si 19.9 mJ/m² (−6.4). Linear SFE coefficients are -2.6 (Si) and 4.9 (Al) mJ/m²/wt%. Al raises SFE, suppressing shear bands. Key figures/tables: Table 1 (compositions), Table 2 (coefficients), Figure 1 (dislocation micrograph), Figure 2 (lattice parameter), Figure 3 (dislocation spacing and SFE). Subsequent chunks should retain the alloy aliases, base property values, and the noted SFE trends.
processing_route: TEM preparation: grinding, electropolishing in 95% acetic acid + 5% perchloric acid
XRD preparation: grinding to 600 grit, chemical polishing in 10 H2O + 10 H2O2 + 1 HF
microstructure: Dissociated dislocations with stacking faults imaged
properties: Upper limit corrected SFE for 2.5 Al: 44.0 ± 9.8 mJ/m²
mechanisms: Al expands austenite lattice, Si negligible
Al increases SFE, Si decreases SFE
SFE increase suppresses shear band formation and strain-induced martensite
Ignoring Al in XRD-based carbon measurement overestimates C content
Linear additive relationships assumed for composition effects on a0 and SFE
figures_or_tables: Figure 1: WBDF of dissociated dislocation in base
Figure 3a: partial dislocation spacing vs angle
Table 1: alloy compositions and annealing temperatures
Table 2: lattice parameter and SFE coefficients

rolling_context_summary:
Summary: This chunk presents core experimental data for the effects of 2.5 wt.% Si and Al on austenite lattice parameter and SFE in three fully austenitic Fe-Ni-Cr alloys: Base, 2.5 Si, and 2.5 Al. Processing included vacuum casting, homogenization (1200°C/20 h), hot rolling, and annealing to ~140 μm grain size. XRD (Cu Kα, extrapolation) yielded lattice parameters: base 0.3585 nm, 2.5 Si 0.3587 nm, 2.5 Al 0.3598 nm, corresponding to a 1.41×10⁻³ nm expansion by Al and negligible Si effect. Linear lattice parameter coefficients were derived; ignoring Al leads to carbon content overestimation of 0.125–0.176 wt.% for 1 wt.% Al. SFE was determined by WBDF TEM of dissociated dislocations in 5% pre-strained samples; uncorrected SFE values: base 26.3 mJ/m², 2.5 Al 38.3 mJ/m² (+12.0), 2.5 Si 19.9 mJ/m² (−6.4). Linear SFE coefficients are -2.6 (Si) and 4.9 (Al) mJ/m²/wt%. Al raises SFE, suppressing shear bands. Key figures/tables: Table 1 (compositions), Table 2 (coefficients), Figure 1 (dislocation micrograph), Figure 2 (lattice parameter), Figure 3 (dislocation spacing and SFE). Subsequent chunks should retain the alloy aliases, base property values, and the noted SFE trends.
Material systems: Fe-Ni-Cr austenitic steel; Base alloy (Fe-15.05Ni-11.06Cr-1.09Mn-0.028C-0.002Si-0.05Al); 2.5 Si alloy (Fe-14.99Ni-10.98Cr-1.10Mn-0.028C-2.50Si-0.06Al); 2.5 Al alloy (Fe-15.04Ni-11.02Cr-1.08Mn-0.030C-0.034Si-2.47Al)
Sample aliases: Base; 2.5 Si; 2.5 Al
Processing route: Vacuum casting; Homogenization: 1200 °C, 20 h; Hot rolling; Annealing: 1 h at 1040 °C (Base), 1075 °C (2.5 Si), 1060 °C (2.5 Al); 5% true compression for SFE samples; TEM preparation: grinding, electropolishing in 95% acetic acid + 5% perchloric acid; XRD preparation: grinding to 600 grit, chemical polishing in 10 H2O + 10 H2O2 + 1 HF
Key variables: Alloy composition (Si, Al); Solution annealing temperature; Austenite lattice parameter a0; Stacking fault energy (SFE); Partial dislocation separation d; Dislocation character angle α; Lattice parameter coefficients A_LP; SFE coefficients A_SFE
Microstructure: Fully austenitic single phase; Coarse grain size ≈140 μm; 5% pre-strain introduces dislocations; Dissociated dislocations with stacking faults imaged
Properties: Base a0 = 0.3585 nm; 2.5 Si a0 = 0.3587 nm (minimal change); 2.5 Al a0 = 0.3598 nm (+1.41×10⁻³ nm); Base SFE = 26.3 mJ/m²; 2.5 Si SFE = 19.9 mJ/m² (Δ = -6.4); 2.5 Al SFE = 38.3 mJ/m² (Δ = +12.0); Upper limit corrected SFE for 2.5 Al: 44.0 ± 9.8 mJ/m²; A_LP(Si) = -0.018×10⁻³ nm/wt%, A_LP(Al) = 0.582×10⁻³ nm/wt%
Mechanisms: Al expands austenite lattice, Si negligible; Al increases SFE, Si decreases SFE; SFE increase suppresses shear band formation and strain-induced martensite; Ignoring Al in XRD-based carbon measurement overestimates C content; Linear additive relationships assumed for composition effects on a0 and SFE
Figures or tables: Figure 1: WBDF of dissociated dislocation in base; Figure 2: lattice parameter vs alloying; Figure 3a: partial dislocation spacing vs angle; Figure 3b: SFE vs alloying; Table 1: alloy compositions and annealing temperatures; Table 2: lattice parameter and SFE coefficients
